title: zentinel pipeline context packet v1
description: The single durable input handed to a fresh stateless subagent before it performs a pipeline role. A packet is self-contained: it names the task, the role, the files the role may and may not touch, the docs and prior artifacts it needs, the constraints and verification expectations it must honor, and the conditions under which it must stop.

schema_version.description: Pinned schema identity for this artifact.

packet_id.description: Stable identifier for the packet, conventionally <task-id>-<role-slug>-<seq> (for example 043-mutation-agent-001). Used by stale-context markers to name the packet they supersede.

created_from_queue_revision.description: Queue revision the packet was generated from. A subagent must treat the packet as stale if the live queue revision no longer matches.

task.description: Identity of the active task the role is working on.

file.description: Repository-relative path to the task spec file.

role.description: Pipeline role the packet is scoped to (for example Test Author, Implementer, Mutation Agent, Doctest Agent, Verifier). Exactly one role per packet.

allowed_files.description: Files the role is permitted to create or modify. An empty array means the role modifies no tracked files (review and analysis roles).

forbidden_files.description: Files the role must not modify even though they are relevant context. Requiring a forbidden file is a stop condition.

relevant_docs.description: Repository-relative doc paths the role should read. Prefer stable references over pasted bulk content.

prior_artifacts.description: Paths to prior pipeline artifacts (handoffs, reports) the role depends on. A referenced artifact that is missing is a stop condition.

known_constraints.description: Hard constraints the role must honor (for example deterministic core must not call AI, do not edit approved tests).

verification_expectations.description: Concrete checks the role's output must satisfy (for example exact commands that must pass or fail).

handoff_required.description: Whether the role must emit a handoff artifact before the pipeline advances.

stop_conditions.description: Deterministic conditions under which the role must stop and report rather than proceed (for example task state changed, forbidden file required, required prior artifact missing).
